editor_note: The interesting move was not cloning Calendly in public. It was treating scheduling as infrastructure: a self-hosted option, API hooks, and a hosted product for teams that do not want to run it themselves. That is a sharper bet than generic booking software. Cal.com is selling control, not just calendar pages.
investors: Dick Costolo, Joseph Jacks (OSS Capital), Peer Richelsen

product_evidence.claim: Official product positioning describes Cal.com: Open source scheduling infrastructure.
It was treating scheduling as infrastructure: a self-hosted option, API hooks, and a hosted product for teams that do not want to run it themselves.

market_context.primary_user: Business teams evaluating workflow software for a specific operating function.
market_context.why_now: Recent source-backed funding and public product evidence make scheduling, open source, calendar worth tracking.

risks.claim: Continued tracking should verify whether the public product evidence turns into durable customer adoption beyond the currently visible surface.
risks.basis: VentureDex editorial assessment based on official product evidence and funding-source review.
